creator: nwhitehorn@FreeBSD.org
text: That makes sense. Most UEFI systems do at least allow CSM booting, so just banning it is indeed not the right choice. I'll try to get that in tomorrow. Longer term, what we really need is ZFS support in the UEFI bootblocks (ideally, we could also use the same bootblocks for BIOS for UFS and ZFS, but maybe that's a pipe dream).
